ground_truth.description: Photo of one of the "towers" in Wat Arun in Bangkok, looking up. Nothing else visible. Should be easy.
ground_truth.photographer_description: A photo of one of the «towers» (prang) in Wat Arun in Bangkok, looking up. Nothing else is visible. You can see the porcelain decoration on the tower. Clear sky, daytime/close to sunset. This should be easy to get.

details.description: The image shows the richly ornamented prang towers of Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) in Bangkok at golden hour. Dominating the scene is the central Khmer-style prang with steep tiered terraces, covered in dense stucco reliefs and inlaid with multicolored Chinese porcelain and seashell mosaics forming floral and geometric motifs. Rows of mythological figures (yaksha and kinnari/monkey guardians) support the tiers. A steep white staircase with black riser stripes leads up the central prang. To the right stands a smaller satellite prang with similarly intricate porcelain decoration and a tall spire. The warm low-angle sunlight enhances the textures and colors against a clear blue sky. Visitors are visible at the base and on the stairs, and modern lights and balustrades frame the lower edges, indicating a temple courtyard setting.

details.description: The image features two elaborately decorated Thai temple towers (prangs) set against a clear blue sky. On the left is a tall, multi-tiered central prang with intricate ceramic tile mosaics and numerous small figures along its base, typical of Wat Arun's Khmer-influenced design. To the right is a shorter, ornate sanctuary tower with a pointed spire and richly patterned tile work. Stone staircases and ornamental balustrades lead up to the platforms where visitors are strolling. The overall scene exudes the decorative grandeur and religious significance of Wat Arun in Bangkok, with warm evening light enhancing the glint of ceramic shards and gilded accents.

details.description: Low-angle photo at golden hour of Wat Arun’s ornate prang towers in Bangkok. The massive central Khmer-style prang dominates the frame, clad in intricate floral porcelain and seashell mosaics with rows of mythic guardian figures. A steep white-and-black staircase rises to mid-level terraces. To the right stands a smaller, richly decorated prang or mondop-like structure with gilded details and lattice windows. Visitors are visible at the base; sky is clear blue.

details.description: A daytime exterior view of Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) in Bangkok, Thailand. The scene features the temple’s towering central prang and a gilded-adorned, ornate secondary prang, both covered in intricate porcelain mosaic decorations. Stairs and decorative balustrades lead up to the base, with visitors visible around the platform. The sky is clear and blue, highlighting the detailed ceramic inlays and the temple’s ornate architectural style.

details.description: A low-angle, upward view of the majestic Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) in Bangkok, bathed in warm sunlight against a clear blue sky. The image is dominated by the towering central prang (spire) on the left, showcasing its intricate architectural details and colorful floral mosaics made from Chinese porcelain and seashells. To the right stands a smaller satellite prang, similarly adorned. The structures feature steep staircases and multiple tiers supported by rows of sculpted mythical figures. Visitors can be seen walking along the terraces and base of the temple.

details.description: A low-angle shot of the iconic Phra Prang (central tower) and a satellite prang of Wat Arun Ratchawararam Ratchaworamahawihan in Bangkok. The structures are built in a Khmer-influenced architectural style, characterized by their towering, corn-cob-shaped spires. The surfaces are intricately decorated with thousands of pieces of colorful Chinese porcelain shards and seashells, forming floral patterns and figures of mythical beings like Kinnari and Yaksha. Steep stone staircases lead up the sides of the main prang, with tourists visible on the lower levels. The scene is bathed in the warm, golden light of late afternoon, casting long shadows and highlighting the texture of the porcelain mosaics against a clear, pale blue sky.

details.description: The image shows the ornate towers of Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) in Bangkok at golden hour. Dominating the scene is the tall central prang, a Khmer-style tower clad in stucco embedded with colorful porcelain and seashell mosaics, with tiers of sculpted guardian figures and floral motifs. A steep staircase climbs the base terraces where visitors are ascending. To the right stands a smaller accompanying tower with an elongated spire and decorative lattice windows, similarly covered in intricate ceramic inlay. The clear sky emphasizes the vertical silhouettes and gilded finials of the spires.

details.description: The image shows a section of Wat Arun, the Temple of Dawn in Bangkok. The central prang dominates the left side, rising in a series of stacked, tapering tiers richly decorated with intricate ceramic tile mosaics and sculptural details. To the right, a secondary ornate tower with a tall, slender spire features vertically oriented panels and richly tiled surfaces. Both structures sit on white stepped bases and are adorned with colorful glaze patterns in blues, greens, creams, and golds, reflecting light in a warm late-afternoon glow. A few visitors are visible at the base and along the staircases, providing scale to the monumental architecture. The overall style is Thai Buddhist temple architecture with Khmer-style prang elements and extensive porcelain mosaic decoration.

details.description: A grand riverside Thai Buddhist temple complex with an enormous intricately tiled central prang (Khmer-style stepped tower) covered in colorful porcelain and stucco figures. The tower’s terraces are ringed with guardian and celestial sculptures, and steep stairways lead upward. At right stands a smaller but highly ornate structure with lattice windows and a tall spire, all similarly encrusted with floral porcelain mosaics. Visitors are seen on the terraces, and the scene is lit by warm late-afternoon sunlight under a clear blue sky. This is Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) in Bangkok, Thailand, viewed from within the temple courtyard looking up toward the central prang.

details.description: A low-angle, vertical photograph capturing the majestic architecture of Wat Arun (The Temple of Dawn) in Bangkok, Thailand. The image is dominated by the massive central prang (spire) on the left, which rises steeply towards a clear, gradient blue sky. To the right, a smaller, intricately detailed satellite tower (likely a mondop or smaller prang) stands adjacent. Both structures are encrusted with the temple's signature colorful Chinese porcelain mosaics and seashells arranged in complex floral and geometric patterns. The central prang features multiple tiered levels supported by rows of sculpted figures, appearing to be Yakshas or demons acting as caryatids. At the base, a white staircase with a railing leads up to a terrace where several tourists are visible, providing scale to the monumental structures. The lighting is warm and golden, suggesting late afternoon, highlighting the texture and vibrant colors of the architectural ornamentation.
